title: Command Inspector Review Console
concept: A command-palette-first Playground redesign for core contributors reviewing PRs: the omnibar and grouped command results are the primary discovery surface, while every current action opens a compact contextual inspector drawer beside the live WordPress preview.
focus: command palette as the core navigation and discovery model
top navigation with contextual inspector drawer
route-specific PR, GitHub, Blueprint URL, zip, and vanilla launch inputs
compact enterprise SaaS styling with thin borders
flows: Search or filter commands to start Vanilla WordPress, preview a WordPress PR, preview a Gutenberg PR or branch, import from GitHub, run a Blueprint URL, or import a zip with distinct route constraints.
Save a temporary Playground to browser storage or to a local directory with separate destination copy progress and resulting saved identity.
Manage saved and unsaved Playgrounds from the inspector, including open, rename, delete, and temporary-state warnings.
Use the Site Manager commands for settings reset/reload, file browser editing, current Blueprint copy/download/run, database tools, and logs while the active WordPress preview remains visible.
Browse a credible Blueprint catalog with search, category filters, 1-12 of 43 density, selected detail, inspect, and run behavior.
Export to GitHub, download a zip, download database.sqlite, import a zip over the current site, and download the current Blueprint bundle.
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
refresh the active embedded WordPress page
use File browser with create folder/file, upload, browse, and code editing affordances
